description: Create the branch, install twilio dependency, add types, build the CallSessionManager state machine, TwiML builder, TwilioPhoneProvider (dual-mode), NativePhoneProvider fallback, and CallAgent. Then add comprehensive tests for all components.
steps: 1. Create and switch to branch feat/phase-42-twilio-sip from the current branch.
2. Add 'twilio' (^5.0.0) to packages/device-control/package.json dependencies, then run pnpm install.

3. Create packages/device-control/src/providers/types.ts with: CallState enum/type (idle, dialing, ringing, connected, on-hold, ended, failed), CallSession interface (callSid, status, fromNumber, toNumber, startTime, duration, direction: inbound|outbound), CallEvent interface (type: state-change|dtmf|error, callSid, data), TwilioConfig interface (accountSid, authToken, fromNumber, statusCallbackUrl?), CallAgentIntent interface (action: place|answer|end|hold|transfer, target?, callId?).

4. Create packages/device-control/src/providers/call-session.ts with CallSessionManager class: tracks Map<string, CallSession>, methods: create(callSid, from, to, direction) -> CallSession, transition(callSid, newState) with valid transition checks (idle->dialing->ringing->connected->on-hold->ended, any->failed, connected<->on-hold), get(callSid), getActive() returns all non-ended sessions, end(callSid), onStateChange(callback) event listener pattern using a simple callback array.

5. Create packages/device-control/src/providers/twiml-builder.ts with TwiMLBuilder class: static methods that return XML strings: dial(number, callerId?) -> <Response><Dial callerId>number</Dial></Response>, say(message, voice?) -> <Response><Say voice>message</Say></Response>, hold(musicUrl?) -> <Response><Play loop=0>musicUrl</Play></Response> or <Response><Say>Please hold</Say><Pause length=60/></Response>, conference(name, options?) -> <Response><Dial><Conference>name</Conference></Dial></Response>, gather(options: {action, numDigits?, timeout?}) -> <Response><Gather action numDigits timeout/></Response>.

6. Create packages/device-control/src/providers/twilio-phone-provider.ts implementing PhoneCapability: constructor(config?: TwilioConfig) - if config has accountSid+authToken+fromNumber or env vars TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID/TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN/TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER, set liveMode=true and create Twilio client. placeCall(number) - in live mode: call twilioClient.calls.create({to, from, url: statusCallbackUrl}), track via CallSessionManager, return callSid. In mock mode: generate fake callSid, simulate state transitions. endCall(callSid) - in live mode: update call status to completed. holdCall(callSid) - transition to on-hold. transferCall(callSid, target) - in live mode: create conference bridge. answerCall(callSid) - transition to connected. isAvailable() returns true, initialize() is no-op, dispose() ends all active sessions.

7. Create packages/device-control/src/providers/native-phone-provider.ts implementing PhoneCapability: placeCall(number) opens tel: URI (returns a generated callId), answerCall/endCall/holdCall/transferCall all throw 'Not supported in native dialer mode'. isAvailable() returns true, initialize()/dispose() are no-ops. This is a fire-and-forget fallback.

8. Create packages/device-control/src/agents/call-agent.ts with CallAgent class: constructor takes { phoneProvider: PhoneCapability, permissionManager: PermissionManager, allowedContacts?: string[] }. Method handleIntent(intent: CallAgentIntent) -> Promise<{success, callSid?, error?}>: validates permission tier (tier 2 for allowlisted contacts, tier 3 for unknown numbers), checks permission state, delegates to phoneProvider methods.
9. Create packages/device-control/src/providers/index.ts barrel export for all providers.
10. Update packages/device-control/src/index.ts to export from './providers/index.js' and './agents/call-agent.js'.

11. Create packages/device-control/src/__tests__/call-session.test.ts: test state machine transitions (valid transitions succeed, invalid transitions throw), concurrent sessions, event callbacks on state change.
12. Create packages/device-control/src/__tests__/twiml-builder.test.ts: test XML output for dial, say, hold, conference, gather - verify correct XML structure.

13. Create packages/device-control/src/__tests__/twilio-phone-provider.test.ts: test mock mode (no credentials): placeCall returns a callSid, endCall transitions state, holdCall transitions state. Test that constructor detects live mode from config. Mock the twilio module to test live mode call creation.

14. Create packages/device-control/src/__tests__/call-agent.test.ts: test intent parsing, permission checks (denied permission blocks call), allowlist logic (tier 2 for known contacts, tier 3 for unknown).
15. Run pnpm --filter @quant/device-control typecheck and pnpm --filter @quant/device-control test to verify everything passes.
16. Stage and commit with message: feat(device-control): add Twilio SIP phone provider with call agent
acceptance_criteria: Branch feat/phase-42-twilio-sip exists and is checked out
TwilioPhoneProvider implements PhoneCapability and works in mock mode without credentials
TwilioPhoneProvider works in live mode when Twilio credentials are provided (tested with mocked twilio client)
CallSessionManager correctly enforces state machine transitions
TwiMLBuilder generates valid XML for dial, say, hold, conference, gather
NativePhoneProvider opens tel: URI and throws on unsupported operations
CallAgent checks permissions and delegates to phone provider
pnpm --filter @quant/device-control typecheck passes with zero errors
pnpm --filter @quant/device-control test passes all tests including new ones
Total new code is within 500 LOC guidance
findings: Used injectable TwilioClientLike interface instead of vi.mock('twilio') to properly test live mode - the real twilio SDK cannot be module-mocked with require(). Pre-commit hook ran prettier which reformatted files; all tests still pass after formatting. 49 total tests (32 new + 17 existing). Lint-staged added ~120 LOC of formatting changes to the lockfile diff.
